methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $54M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
DeepDrive, a Series B transportation-tech company founded by Felix Pörnbacher and Alexander Rosen, has raised $54.4M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a private-company comp universe of 8 firms in the transportation sector, including Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M). No direct public comparables exist in this space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $54.4M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $103M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps yields a median valuation of $500M, with a range of $270M to $1.8B. The peer-median method carries greater weight given its cross-market grounding, though the wide spread ($270M–$1.8B) reflects material stage and trajectory variance within the comp set.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $88M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $302M. Key uncertainties include whether DeepDrive's market position and growth trajectory align with the upper-stage peers (Einride, Harbinger) or whether earlier-stage comparables (REGENT Craft) better reflect current execution risk. The wide peer dispersion underscores sensitivity to assumptions about competitive positioning and path to capital efficiency.
